title: The New PM, Claire Vo | Compile 26
summary: Claire Vo, founder of ChatPRD, discusses how product management must evolve when code is abundant but market demand is scarce. She argues that PM work shifts from feasibility constraints to discovery and validation of what people actually want, requiring new approaches to ideation and market fit.
writeup: Claire Vo, founder of ChatPRD, argues that product management changes once code becomes abundant and the binding constraint shifts to market demand. The talk reframes PM work away from feasibility and engineering limits toward discovery and validation of what people actually want, calling for new methods in ideation and market fit. The core takeaway: when building is cheap, the scarce skill is finding and confirming real demand rather than judging what can be built.
